title: launaea fragilis subsp. pumila
intro: Launaea fragilis subsp. pumila, also known as the dwarf desert lettuce, is a plant species that belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae) and is native to the deserts of North Africa. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been traditionally employed in the treatment of various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Launaea fragilis subsp. pumila include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and dried extracts, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
